title: Indoor Room Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The indoor unit's room temperature sensor (thermistor) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The CH01 error code indicates the indoor unit's room temperature sensor (thermistor) is providing abnormal readings, is shorted, or is open. This sensor measures the ambient room temperature and is critical for the AC to determine when to cycle on and off. Without accurate readings, the unit cannot maintain the set temperature.

causes.cause: Loose or corroded sensor wiring connection
Sensor displaced from its proper mounting position
Indoor PCB sensor input circuit failure

symptoms: CH01 displayed on the indoor unit or remote
AC runs continuously without cycling off
AC does not start cooling
Room temperature display is incorrect
Unit short-cycles (turns on and off rapidly)

fixSteps.title: Reset the AC unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the AC at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Turn it back on and see if the error clears. Transient sensor glitches may resolve with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor placement
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel of the indoor unit. Locate the room temperature sensor (a small probe usually mounted near the air intake grille). Ensure it is securely in its clip and not touching the evaporator coils or hanging loose.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring from the probe to the PCB connector. Ensure the connector is firmly seated. Look for damaged or pinched wires.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor from the PCB. Using a multimeter, measure resistance: at 77F (25C) it should read approximately 10,000 ohms (10k NTC). If the reading is 0, infinite, or significantly off, replace the sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor replacement does not resolve the error, the indoor PCB may need diagnosis. For mini-split systems, HVAC technician access is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH01 room temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Can I still use the AC with CH01?
faq.a: The AC may run but will not regulate temperature correctly. It may run continuously or not at all, resulting in discomfort and higher energy bills.

faq.q: What type of sensor does LG use?
faq.a: LG air conditioners typically use NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) thermistors. Their resistance decreases as temperature increases.

faq.q: Is CH01 common?
faq.a: Temperature sensor failures are among the more common AC issues, especially in older units. The sensors are inexpensive and relatively easy to replace.

title: Indoor Pipe (Coil) Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The evaporator coil temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The CH02 error code indicates the indoor evaporator coil temperature sensor (pipe sensor) has failed. This sensor monitors the temperature of the refrigerant in the evaporator coils to prevent freezing, control defrost cycles, and manage compressor operation. It is critical for preventing coil icing and ensuring efficient cooling.

causes.cause: Sensor displaced from its mounting on the coil

symptoms: CH02 displayed on the unit
Evaporator coils freezing over with ice
AC not cooling effectively
Compressor cycling erratically
Water dripping from the indoor unit

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restart and monitor for the error.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor mounting
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel and access the evaporator coils. The pipe sensor should be firmly clipped or taped to a specific location on the evaporator. If it has fallen off, reattach it to the coil.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor from the PCB. At room temperature (77F), it should read approximately 10,000 ohms. When placed against the cold coils, resistance should increase (NTC behavior). Replace if readings are abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor from the coil. Install the new one in the exact same position on the coil, securing it with the clip or thermal tape provided. Reconnect to the PCB.

whenToCallPro: For mini-split and central systems where the evaporator is not easily accessible.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH02 pipe coil sensor error fix

faq.q: Why are my coils freezing?
faq.a: Without proper coil temperature monitoring (CH02), the system cannot detect when coils are getting too cold and does not initiate a defrost cycle.

faq.q: Can a dirty filter cause CH02?
faq.a: A dirty filter does not cause the sensor to fail, but restricted airflow from a dirty filter can cause ice buildup that may damage or displace the sensor.

faq.q: Is the pipe sensor the same part as the room sensor?
faq.a: Often yes, they use the same NTC thermistor part number. However, they are mounted in different locations and serve different purposes.

title: Indoor/Outdoor Communication Error
shortDescription: The indoor and outdoor units cannot communicate with each other.
fullDescription: The CH05 error code indicates a communication failure between the indoor and outdoor units of a split or multi-split AC system. The two units communicate through a signal wire in the control cable. This error can be caused by wiring issues, a faulty PCB on either unit, incorrect wiring, or electromagnetic interference.

causes.cause: Loose or disconnected communication wiring between units
Damaged signal wire in the interconnecting cable

symptoms: CH05 displayed on the indoor unit
Outdoor unit does not start when indoor unit calls for cooling
System will not operate at all
Indoor unit fan runs but no cooling or heating
Error appears shortly after startup

fixSteps.title: Reset both units
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker that powers both indoor and outdoor units for 5 minutes. Turn it back on and allow 3 minutes for the units to initialize before starting.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open the electrical compartment on both the indoor and outdoor units. Check that all wiring connections (typically 3 or 4 wires) are secure at the terminal blocks on both ends. Look for loose screws or disconnected wires.

fixSteps.title: Test the communication wire
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the communication wire at both ends. Using a multimeter, test continuity of each wire end-to-end. Any wire showing no continuity is broken and the cable needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check voltage at outdoor unit
fixSteps.instruction: With power restored, measure the voltage at the outdoor unit terminal block. Proper voltage should be present (typically 220-240V between L1-L2 or L-N). If no voltage is reaching the outdoor unit, check the dedicated circuit breaker and wiring.

whenToCallPro: If wiring checks out fine, a PCB on either unit may need replacement. This requires HVAC technician diagnosis. Also if the system was recently installed, the installer should check their wiring.
videoSearchQuery: LG mini split CH05 communication error fix indoor outdoor

faq.q: Does CH05 affect window AC units?
faq.a: No, CH05 is specific to split and multi-split systems that have separate indoor and outdoor units connected by control wiring.

faq.q: Can weather damage cause CH05?
faq.a: Yes, lightning strikes, flooding, or rodent damage to the outdoor wiring can cause communication failures between units.

faq.q: Is this a common error after installation?
faq.a: Yes, CH05 is one of the most common post-installation errors, usually caused by incorrect wiring. Verify that the wires are connected to matching terminals on both units.

title: Indoor Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The indoor blower fan motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The CH10 error code indicates the indoor blower fan motor has failed or is not running at the expected speed. The indoor fan is responsible for blowing air across the evaporator coils and into the room. Without it, the AC cannot distribute conditioned air and the coils will freeze.

causes.cause: Blocked or dirty fan wheel preventing rotation
Fan motor capacitor failure (on AC motors)
Indoor PCB not sending proper voltage to fan

symptoms: CH10 displayed on the unit
No air blowing from the indoor unit
Indoor unit is very quiet (no fan sound)
Evaporator coils freezing over
Water dripping from the unit due to ice melting

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restart and check if the fan operates. A temporary motor overload may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open the front panel and remove the filters. The blower wheel (squirrel cage fan) may be heavily clogged with dust. Clean it with a soft brush or compressed air. Heavy buildup can prevent the motor from spinning.

fixSteps.title: Check for physical obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: With the panel open, try to spin the fan wheel by hand. It should rotate freely. If it is stuck or grinds, there may be a foreign object or the motor bearings may have seized.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the fan motor wiring at the PCB. Test the motor windings with a multimeter for continuity. If the motor has a capacitor, test it as well. Replace the motor if it fails testing.

whenToCallPro: Fan motor replacement in mini-split indoor units can be complex due to tight spaces. Professional service is recommended for split systems.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH10 indoor fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can a dirty filter cause CH10?
faq.a: A dirty filter restricts airflow but does not directly cause the fan motor to fail. However, the extra strain on the motor from pushing air through a clogged filter can accelerate motor wear.

faq.q: How often should I clean the fan wheel?
faq.a: Clean the blower wheel at least once a year, or more often in dusty environments. Regular filter cleaning (monthly) helps keep the fan wheel cleaner.

faq.q: Can the fan motor be repaired?
faq.a: Fan motors are typically replaced as complete units rather than repaired. The cost of the motor is relatively low compared to labor.

title: Outdoor DC Compressor Current Error
shortDescription: The outdoor compressor is drawing abnormal electrical current.
fullDescription: The CH21 error code indicates the outdoor unit's compressor is drawing excessive or abnormal DC current. In inverter-driven systems, the compressor motor is powered by a DC inverter board that precisely controls speed. Overcurrent can indicate a compressor winding issue, refrigerant problem, or inverter board failure.

causes.cause: Low refrigerant charge causing abnormal pressures
Blocked or dirty condenser coils causing high pressure

symptoms: CH21 displayed on the indoor unit
Outdoor unit shuts down shortly after starting
System not cooling or heating
Outdoor unit makes unusual buzzing or clicking sounds
Circuit breaker trips when outdoor unit starts

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Use a garden hose to gently spray the outdoor unit's condenser coils from the inside out to remove dirt, leaves, and debris. Dirty coils cause high pressure and overcurrent. Allow to dry before restarting.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the outdoor unit has at least 24 inches of clearance on all sides. Remove any plants, fences, or debris that restrict airflow to the condenser.

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for the outdoor unit for 10 minutes. This resets the inverter board and allows the compressor to cool down. Restart and monitor.

fixSteps.title: Check compressor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open the outdoor unit electrical panel. Check all compressor terminal connections for tightness and signs of arcing or burn marks. Tighten any loose connections.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the condenser and resetting does not resolve CH21, call an HVAC technician. Compressor and inverter board diagnosis requires specialized equipment.
videoSearchQuery: LG mini split CH21 compressor overcurrent error fix

faq.q: Is CH21 a compressor failure?
faq.a: Not necessarily. CH21 indicates overcurrent which can be caused by dirty coils, low refrigerant, or an inverter board issue. A technician can determine the exact cause.

faq.q: Can I clean the outdoor coils myself?
faq.a: Yes, gentle rinsing with a garden hose from inside out is safe and effective. Avoid using a pressure washer as it can damage the delicate fins.

faq.q: How much does inverter board replacement cost?
faq.a: Inverter boards typically cost $100-300 for the part, plus $100-200 for professional installation.

title: Inverter Compressor Current Sensor Error
shortDescription: The current sensor on the inverter compressor board is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: The CH22 error code indicates the current sensor on the inverter board that monitors compressor current draw is providing abnormal readings or has failed. This sensor is essential for the inverter board to precisely control the compressor motor speed and protect it from overcurrent damage.

causes.cause: Faulty current sensor on the inverter board
Loose connections on the inverter board

symptoms: CH22 displayed on the unit
Compressor does not start
System shuts down intermittently
Outdoor unit attempts to start but immediately stops

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes to fully discharge the inverter board capacitors. Restart and monitor for the error.

fixSteps.title: Check supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, verify that the incoming voltage to the outdoor unit is within the rated range (typically 198-264V for 220V systems). Low or high voltage can cause inverter board malfunctions.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inverter board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power and wait 5 minutes for capacitors to discharge. Open the outdoor unit panel and check all connections on the inverter board for looseness, corrosion, or burn marks.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inverter board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the inverter board likely needs replacement. This requires professional HVAC service as it involves high-voltage components and may require system commissioning after replacement.

whenToCallPro: CH22 almost always requires professional HVAC service due to the inverter board complexity.
videoSearchQuery: LG mini split CH22 inverter current sensor error

faq.q: Is CH22 dangerous?
faq.a: The overcurrent protection system is working as designed by detecting the issue and shutting down. Do not bypass or ignore this error.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause CH22?
faq.a: Yes, voltage spikes can damage inverter board components. A surge protector on the AC circuit is recommended.

faq.q: How long do inverter boards last?
faq.a: Inverter boards typically last 8-15 years. Voltage issues and power surges can shorten their lifespan.

title: DC Link Low Voltage Error
shortDescription: The DC link voltage in the inverter circuit is below the acceptable range.
fullDescription: The CH23 error code indicates the DC link voltage in the outdoor unit's inverter circuit has dropped below the minimum threshold. The inverter converts AC power to DC and then back to AC at variable frequency to control the compressor speed. Low DC link voltage prevents proper compressor operation.

causes.cause: Faulty inverter board DC bus capacitors
Undersized power wiring causing voltage drop

symptoms: CH23 displayed on the unit
Compressor will not start
System attempts to start then shuts down
Error appears during peak electrical demand periods

fixSteps.title: Check supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the incoming AC voltage at the outdoor unit disconnect with a multimeter while the unit is trying to start. Voltage should be within 10% of the rated voltage (198-242V for 220V systems).

fixSteps.title: Check all power connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Inspect all power wire connections from the disconnect to the outdoor unit terminal block. Tighten any loose connections. Check wire gauge is appropriate for the circuit length.

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes. This allows the DC bus capacitors to discharge fully. Restart and test.

fixSteps.title: Have voltage and wiring inspected
fixSteps.instruction: If the supply voltage is consistently low, contact your electric utility or an electrician to address the power quality issue. The unit cannot function properly with inadequate voltage.

whenToCallPro: CH23 requires voltage measurement under load which should be performed by a qualified HVAC technician or electrician.
videoSearchQuery: LG mini split CH23 DC link voltage error fix

faq.q: Why does CH23 appear during hot afternoons?
faq.a: During peak demand periods (hot afternoons), utility voltage can sag as many air conditioners and appliances run simultaneously. This voltage drop triggers CH23.

faq.q: Can a voltage stabilizer help?
faq.a: Yes, an automatic voltage regulator (AVR) or stabilizer can help maintain consistent voltage to the AC unit, preventing CH23 in areas with unstable power.

faq.q: Is the wiring size important?
faq.a: Yes, undersized wiring causes voltage drop, especially over long runs. Verify the wire gauge matches LG's specifications for the distance between the breaker and the outdoor unit.

title: Outdoor Inverter Compressor Overload
shortDescription: The inverter compressor has exceeded its maximum current limit.
fullDescription: The CH26 error code indicates the inverter compressor has exceeded its maximum safe current limit and the protection system has shut it down. This is a critical protection to prevent compressor damage. The overcurrent could be due to a mechanical issue with the compressor, abnormal refrigerant pressures, or an electrical fault.

causes.cause: Severely dirty or blocked condenser coils

symptoms: CH26 displayed on the unit
Compressor shuts down after running briefly
Outdoor unit very hot to the touch
High head pressure (can be checked by technician)
System not cooling or heating

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Thoroughly clean the outdoor unit condenser coils with a garden hose, spraying from inside out. Remove all dirt, leaves, grass clippings, and cottonwood seeds from the coil fins.

fixSteps.title: Ensure adequate clearance
fixSteps.instruction: Verify 24 inches of clearance on all sides of the outdoor unit and unrestricted airflow from the top. Remove any covers, plants, or structures blocking airflow.

fixSteps.title: Allow compressor to cool
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, leave the system off for at least 30 minutes to allow the compressor to cool down before restarting.

fixSteps.title: Professional refrigerant check
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after cleaning, have an HVAC technician check the refrigerant charge, pressures, and compressor operation. Improper charge is a common cause of compressor overload.

whenToCallPro: If condenser cleaning does not resolve CH26, professional diagnosis is required.
videoSearchQuery: LG mini split CH26 compressor overload error fix

faq.q: Is CH26 an emergency?
faq.a: The protection system is working correctly by shutting down the compressor. However, continued attempts to run an overloaded compressor can cause permanent damage. Address the cause promptly.

faq.q: Can operating in extreme heat cause CH26?
faq.a: Yes, when outdoor temperatures exceed the unit's rated operating range (typically 115F for cooling), the condenser cannot reject heat efficiently and compressor current rises.

faq.q: How much refrigerant should the system have?
faq.a: The correct charge varies by model and line length. This information is on the outdoor unit nameplate. Only a licensed technician with gauges should check or adjust refrigerant.

title: Outdoor PSC (Pressure Switch) Error
shortDescription: The high pressure switch on the outdoor unit has tripped.
fullDescription: The CH27 error code indicates the high pressure switch in the outdoor unit has opened due to excessively high refrigerant pressure. This is a safety device that prevents compressor damage from excessive head pressure. High pressure is typically caused by condenser airflow restrictions, overcharged refrigerant, or a faulty metering device.

causes.cause: Faulty expansion valve or metering device

symptoms: CH27 displayed on the unit
System shuts down during cooling
Outdoor unit runs very loud before shutting down
Hot liquid line (large copper pipe) at outdoor unit
Unit restarts after cooling down but fails again

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Rinse the condenser coils with a garden hose from inside out. This is the most common and simplest fix for high pressure trips.

fixSteps.title: Check outdoor fan
fixSteps.instruction: With the unit running, verify the outdoor fan is spinning at full speed. A slow or non-running fan cannot remove heat from the coils, causing high pressure.

fixSteps.title: Allow system to reset
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, turn off the system for 30 minutes to allow pressures to equalize and the pressure switch to reset. Restart and monitor.

fixSteps.title: Professional pressure check
fixSteps.instruction: If CH27 persists after cleaning, an HVAC technician needs to check refrigerant pressures, the expansion valve, and the pressure switch itself.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the high pressure condition, professional refrigerant system diagnosis is needed.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH27 high pressure switch error fix

faq.q: What is the high pressure switch?
faq.a: It is a safety device that opens when refrigerant pressure on the high side exceeds a safe limit (typically 400-450 PSI). This protects the compressor and refrigerant lines from damage.

faq.q: Can too much refrigerant cause CH27?
faq.a: Yes, an overcharged system will have excessive head pressure, tripping the high pressure switch. Only a technician should adjust refrigerant levels.

faq.q: Why does CH27 happen more in summer?
faq.a: Higher outdoor temperatures make it harder for the condenser to reject heat, naturally raising pressures. Combined with dirty coils, this can push pressures above the safety limit.

title: Outdoor Discharge Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The CH32 error code indicates the compressor discharge temperature sensor (mounted on the discharge line leaving the compressor) has failed or is providing abnormal readings. This sensor prevents the compressor from overheating by monitoring the temperature of the hot gas leaving the compressor.

causes.cause: Sensor displaced from the discharge pipe

symptoms: CH32 displayed on the unit
System shuts down to protect compressor
Compressor runs briefly then stops
No cooling or heating output

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. Restart and monitor. Transient sensor errors may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor placement
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open the outdoor unit panel. Locate the discharge temperature sensor on the hot gas line coming from the compressor. Ensure it is firmly clamped to the pipe and insulated.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring from the sensor to the outdoor PCB. Ensure the connector is firmly seated and the wires are not damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (77F), expect approximately 5,000-10,000 ohms depending on the sensor type. Replace if readings are abnormal.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not resolve the error, the outdoor PCB or a refrigerant system issue may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH32 discharge temperature sensor error

faq.q: What is the discharge temperature?
faq.a: The discharge temperature is the temperature of the hot compressed refrigerant gas as it leaves the compressor. It typically ranges from 150-220F during normal operation.

faq.q: Why is this sensor important?
faq.a: If the discharge temperature gets too high (above 250F), it can break down the refrigerant oil and damage the compressor. This sensor prevents that.

faq.q: Is this sensor the same part as the indoor sensors?
faq.a: Often yes, the same NTC thermistor part number is used, just mounted in a different location.

title: Outdoor Unit Ambient Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The outdoor ambient temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The CH38 error code indicates the outdoor ambient (air) temperature sensor has failed. This sensor measures the outdoor air temperature and helps the system determine optimal compressor speed, defrost timing (in heat pump mode), and operating parameters for efficiency.

causes.cause: Sensor exposed to direct sunlight or radiant heat

symptoms: CH38 displayed on the unit
System may still operate but inefficiently
Heat pump defrost timing incorrect
Compressor speed not optimized for conditions

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restart and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The outdoor ambient sensor is usually mounted on the coil or in the air intake path of the outdoor unit. Check that it is securely mounted and the wiring is intact.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At the current outdoor temperature, the reading should correspond to the NTC chart (10,000 ohms at 77F). Compare to actual temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor from its mounting clip. Install the new sensor in the same position. Reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed for this simple sensor replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH38 outdoor ambient sensor error

faq.q: Can the system still cool with CH38?
faq.a: Often yes, but not as efficiently. The system loses the ability to optimize for outdoor conditions, which can increase energy usage and affect defrost timing in heat mode.

faq.q: Where should the outdoor sensor be mounted?
faq.a: It should be in the air intake path where it reads actual outdoor air temperature, not in direct sunlight or near the hot discharge air from the condenser.

faq.q: Is CH38 urgent?
faq.a: CH38 is a medium-priority error. The system may still operate but repair should be done within a few days for optimal performance.

title: Outdoor Unit EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The outdoor unit control board memory has a read/write error.
fullDescription: The CH44 error code indicates the EEPROM (memory chip) on the outdoor unit's PCB has a read or write error. The EEPROM stores operating parameters, configuration data, and system settings. When it fails, the outdoor unit cannot access the data needed to operate properly.

causes.cause: EEPROM chip failure on the outdoor PCB
PCB component failure affecting EEPROM circuit

symptoms: CH44 displayed on the unit
Outdoor unit will not start
System completely non-functional
No compressor or fan operation

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes to fully discharge all capacitors and allow the EEPROM to reinitialize. Restart and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Check PCB connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open the outdoor unit electrical panel. Inspect all connectors and wiring on the PCB for looseness, corrosion, or moisture damage. Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for moisture damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the PCB for signs of water damage, condensation, or corrosion. Moisture can cause EEPROM and PCB failures. Ensure the electrical compartment cover is properly sealed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the outdoor PCB
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the outdoor PCB needs replacement. This requires professional installation as the PCB may need to be configured for your specific system.

whenToCallPro: CH44 typically requires PCB replacement which should be done by a certified HVAC technician.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH44 EEPROM outdoor PCB error

faq.q: Can a power reset fix CH44?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the EEPROM data was corrupted by a power glitch, a full reset (15 minutes off) can allow it to reinitialize. If the chip itself is damaged, replacement is needed.

faq.q: Can I replace the EEPROM chip instead of the whole PCB?
faq.a: Technically possible for electronics repair specialists, but LG does not support component-level repair. Replacing the entire PCB ensures proper operation.

faq.q: Will the new PCB need programming?
faq.a: Some LG PCBs come pre-programmed for specific model ranges. Others may need DIP switch settings to be configured. The technician will set this up during installation.

title: High Discharge Temperature
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature has exceeded the safe operating limit.
fullDescription: The CH34 error code indicates the compressor discharge gas temperature has exceeded the maximum safe threshold (typically 250F/121C). This is a critical protection error that shuts down the compressor to prevent damage to the compressor windings and refrigerant oil breakdown. High discharge temperature can be caused by low refrigerant, restricted airflow, or compressor issues.

causes.cause: Low refrigerant charge due to a system leak
Dirty or blocked condenser or evaporator coils
Faulty expansion valve not metering refrigerant properly

symptoms: CH34 displayed on the unit
Compressor shuts down during operation
Very hot copper discharge pipe at outdoor unit
Insufficient cooling performance before shutdown
System restarts briefly then trips again

fixSteps.title: Clean all coils
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Clean both the outdoor condenser coils (rinse with hose) and the indoor evaporator (clean or replace filters). Restricted airflow on either side raises discharge temperature.

fixSteps.title: Check airflow
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is blocking the outdoor unit (24 inch clearance), that all indoor supply and return vents are open and unblocked, and that air filters are clean.

fixSteps.title: Allow compressor to cool
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the system for at least 30 minutes before attempting to restart. The compressor needs time to cool down and pressures need to equalize.

fixSteps.title: Call for refrigerant check
fixSteps.instruction: If the error returns after cleaning and resting, low refrigerant is the likely cause. An HVAC technician needs to check pressures, find and repair any leak, and recharge the system.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning coils does not resolve CH34, a refrigerant leak is likely. Leak repair and recharge requires EPA-certified technicians.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH34 high discharge temperature error fix

faq.q: Why is low refrigerant the main cause?
faq.a: Low refrigerant means less mass to absorb heat in the evaporator and less cooling of the compressor. The compressor works harder with less refrigerant, raising discharge temperatures dramatically.

faq.q: Is 250F normal for discharge temperature?
faq.a: Normal discharge temperature ranges from 150-220F depending on conditions. Above 250F, the refrigerant oil begins to break down, which can cause compressor failure.

faq.q: Can I add refrigerant myself?
faq.a: No, handling refrigerant requires EPA Section 608 certification. It is also important to find and fix the leak before adding refrigerant, otherwise it will leak out again.

title: Outdoor Unit PCB Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between the outdoor main PCB and the inverter PCB.
fullDescription: The CH53 error code indicates a communication failure between the outdoor unit's main control PCB and the inverter driver PCB. These two boards work together to control the compressor and outdoor fan. When they cannot communicate, the outdoor unit shuts down entirely.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable or connector between the two boards
Moisture or corrosion on board connectors

symptoms: CH53 displayed on the indoor unit
Outdoor unit completely non-functional
No compressor or outdoor fan operation
Indoor unit runs in fan-only mode

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes. Restart and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Reseat board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power and wait 5 minutes. Open the outdoor unit panel. Disconnect and reconnect all cables between the main PCB and inverter board. Check for corrosion or moisture on connectors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Examine both PCBs for visible damage: burn marks, swollen capacitors, corroded traces, or water staining. Moisture intrusion is a common cause of PCB communication failures.

fixSteps.title: Professional board replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, one or both boards need replacement. An HVAC technician can diagnose which board has failed using test procedures from LG's service manual.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connections does not fix CH53, professional board-level diagnosis is required.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH53 outdoor PCB communication error

faq.q: Are the two outdoor boards covered under warranty?
faq.a: LG typically covers major components including outdoor PCBs under their parts warranty (5-10 years depending on the model). Check your warranty documentation.

faq.q: Can rain or snow cause CH53?
faq.a: If water enters the electrical compartment due to a damaged cover or poor sealing, it can corrode connectors and cause board communication failures. Ensure the panel cover is secure.

faq.q: Do I need to replace both boards?
faq.a: Not necessarily. A technician can determine which board has failed. However, if both are old and one has failed, replacing both may be cost-effective to prevent a second failure.

title: Indoor EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The indoor unit control board memory has a read/write error.
fullDescription: The CH60 error code indicates the EEPROM on the indoor unit's PCB has failed to read or write data correctly. Similar to CH44 for the outdoor unit, this affects the indoor unit's ability to access stored operating parameters and configuration data.

causes.cause: EEPROM chip failure on the indoor PCB
Loose connector on the indoor PCB

symptoms: CH60 displayed on the indoor unit
Indoor unit does not respond to commands
Unit will not start
Display shows incorrect or garbled information

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes. Turn back on and check if the error clears. The EEPROM may reinitialize successfully after a complete power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check PCB connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open the indoor unit panel and inspect all PCB connections and ribbon cables. Reseat any loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the indoor PCB area for signs of moisture, condensation, or water damage. Clean and dry any affected areas.

fixSteps.title: Replace the indoor PCB
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the indoor PCB needs professional replacement. The new board may need configuration for your specific system.

whenToCallPro: CH60 usually requires PCB replacement by a certified technician.
videoSearchQuery: LG air conditioner CH60 indoor EEPROM PCB error

faq.q: Can a power surge cause CH60?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can corrupt EEPROM data. Using a surge protector on the AC circuit helps prevent this.

faq.q: Is CH60 covered under warranty?
faq.a: Check your warranty documentation. LG typically covers the indoor PCB for several years. Contact LG support with your model and serial number.

faq.q: Will a reset fix CH60?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the data was corrupted but the chip is intact, a 15-minute power reset may allow it to reinitialize. If the chip is physically damaged, replacement is needed.

title: Indoor Unit Address Setting Error
shortDescription: The indoor unit's network address is not set correctly for multi-unit systems.
fullDescription: The CH62 error code indicates an address setting conflict or error in a multi-indoor-unit system. In VRF (Variable Refrigerant Flow) or multi-split systems, each indoor unit must have a unique address. Duplicate addresses, unset addresses, or communication issues can trigger this error.

causes.cause: Duplicate indoor unit addresses in the system
Indoor unit address not configured after installation
DIP switch or rotary switch set incorrectly on the PCB

symptoms: CH62 displayed on one or more indoor units
Affected indoor unit does not respond to commands
System cannot identify the indoor unit
Multiple units may show conflicting behavior

fixSteps.title: Check address settings
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit panel and locate the address setting switches (DIP switches or rotary dial) on the PCB. Verify each indoor unit has a unique address number. Consult the installation manual for proper addressing.

fixSteps.title: Resolve duplicate addresses
fixSteps.instruction: If two units have the same address, change one to an unused number. Turn off power before changing any switch settings.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the system
fixSteps.instruction: After correcting addresses, turn off the entire system at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Turn back on and allow all units to initialize (3-5 minutes).

fixSteps.title: Re-commission the system
fixSteps.instruction: Some LG multi-split systems require a re-commissioning procedure after address changes. This is done through the central controller or wired remote. Follow the installation manual procedure.

whenToCallPro: If you are unfamiliar with multi-split system addressing or if the system requires re-commissioning.
videoSearchQuery: LG multi split CH62 address setting error fix VRF

faq.q: Does CH62 affect single-split systems?
faq.a: No, CH62 is specific to multi-split and VRF systems where multiple indoor units connect to one or more outdoor units and need unique addresses.

faq.q: What address numbers are valid?
faq.a: Valid address numbers depend on the system. Typical ranges are 00-15 for residential multi-splits. Consult your specific system's documentation.

faq.q: Can the installer fix this remotely?
faq.a: No, address settings require physical access to the DIP switches on the indoor unit PCB. However, some modern LG systems allow address configuration through the central controller.
